af_score_components.notes: MCP server wrapping GPT Researcher (quality 62). API key for underlying LLM providers. Research report generation takes 2-5 minutes — agents must handle long-running MCP calls. Multiple external API dependencies (LLM + search) create high failure probability. Documentation minimal beyond basic setup. Rate limits from underlying LLM and search providers apply and are often opaque. Not suitable for time-sensitive agent workflows. Better for async batch research use cases.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS to LLM and search providers. API key auth for providers. No scope granularity. Unknown dependency hygiene. API key via environment variable. Multiple provider API keys create credential management complexity. Research queries and results sent to multiple external services — review data policies for sensitive research topics.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. Unknown version — actively developed. No documented breaking change policy. Report generation not idempotent. Long-running operations create timeout and error propagation challenges through MCP layer. Compound failure probability from multiple external APIs. Not suitable for production workflows requiring fast, consistent results.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Grafana API for dashboard and datasource management, alerting, and user management. Service accounts with API keys recommended for agents (replaces legacy API keys). Rate limits documented. Dashboard provisioning via JSON model (complex). Alert rule management via API. Grafana Cloud vs self-hosted have different feature sets. Datasource queries via Explore API. Good for observability automation agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for Grafana Cloud; self-hosted TLS is operator's responsibility. Service accounts with role-based access (Viewer, Editor, Admin). No fine-grained resource-level permissions. Grafana maintained. SOC2 for Grafana Cloud. API key via environment variable. Service account tokens preferred over personal API keys. RBAC plugin available for Enterprise for finer control.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Grafana Cloud has SLA; self-hosted operator-managed. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Dashboard JSON model changes between Grafana versions — version-specific schemas required. Alert rule API changed significantly between Grafana 8 and 9 — check version before integrating. Rate limits on Grafana Cloud vary by plan.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server for Graphlit (multimodal AI content ingestion platform) (quality 62). JWT + API key auth adds complexity. Content ingestion is async — agents must poll completion. Supports documents, images, audio, video ingestion and search. GraphQL API underneath adds query complexity. Rate limits documented. Early-stage platform — API surface may change. Useful for building knowledge-base agents but setup complexity is high.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. JWT + API key dual auth adds credential management complexity. Graphlit maintained. No formal compliance certifications publicly listed. JWT and API key via environment variables. Content ingested to Graphlit servers — review data retention for sensitive documents. GraphQL mutations for content management — scope granularity limited.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with limited public SLA information. Moderate version stability. No formal breaking change policy. Content ingestion async — requires polling for completion status. Ingestion failures may not be clearly reported through MCP layer. GraphQL API changes may require MCP server updates. Early-stage platform carries stability risk for production use.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Groq LPU inference API — extremely fast inference for Llama, Mixtral, and other open models. Simple API key. OpenAI-compatible API format — easy to swap from OpenAI. Rate limits aggressive and per-model (tokens/min, requests/min) — very important to track as Groq's speed makes it easy to hit limits. Free tier generous. Context window varies by model. Streaming supported. Tool use (function calling) available on supported models.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Simple API key — no scope granularity. Groq maintained. No formal compliance certifications listed for inference platform. API key via environment variable. Inference requests sent to Groq servers — data retention policy should be reviewed for sensitive agent prompts. No data training on requests (per policy). Fast inference means high volume queries possible — monitor rate limits closely.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with status page. Stable API versioning (OpenAI-compatible format). Good backward compatibility. Retry guidance documented. Rate limits are aggressive — implement exponential backoff. Different rate limits per model — model selection affects throughput. Free tier rate limits more restrictive than paid. Model availability changes as Groq adds/removes models. Fast inference makes burst rate limit violations common without throttling.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. The Guardian newspaper content API — articles, sections, tags, and metadata. Simple API key passed as query parameter. Free tier: 12 calls/second, 5000/day — adequate for most agent use cases. Full-text search available. Content is high-quality journalism with structured metadata. Article body available with optional extras. Filters by date, section, tag. Curl/HTTP-based — no SDK but very simple. Excellent for news and journalism agent workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Simple API key as query parameter — no scope granularity. Guardian maintained. GDPR compliance as UK media organization. API key can appear in server logs if not handled carefully — prefer passing in header. Read-only news content — no PII or sensitive operations. No formal security certifications listed.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with status page. Stable API — has been running since 2009 with minimal breaking changes. Good backward compatibility. Retry guidance documented. Full GET idempotency on all content queries. Free tier daily limits (5000/day) create hard daily availability boundary. Content is published data — stable once published. Pagination via page parameter. Excellent stability record for a media API.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Gusto HR and payroll platform. OAuth2 only — no API key option makes agent auth complex. Requires refresh token management for long-running agents. Company and employee IDs required for all nested operations. Payroll operations are highly sensitive and irreversible — agents need confirmation flows. Rate limits documented. Partner integration requires Gusto app approval. Important: payroll runs cannot be easily reversed — agents need explicit safeguards.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with payroll-specific scopes (employees:read, payroll:run, etc.). No API key option — OAuth2 required. Gusto maintained. SOC2/GDPR compliance for HR/payroll platform. OAuth tokens via secure flow. Payroll data is highly sensitive (SSN, salary, banking details) — strict access control required. Payroll runs have financial consequences — agents must implement confirmation workflows.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. OAuth token refresh required for long-running agents. Payroll operations not idempotent — retrying can cause duplicate payroll runs. Company ID and employee IDs required at setup. Partner integration approval adds startup time. Payroll processing timing is critical — missed deadlines have financial consequences.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Habitica gamified productivity API — habits, dailies, todos, rewards, guilds. API key uses dual credential: User ID + API Token in headers (x-client header also required). Rate limits documented (30 req/min per user). Task scoring is immediate and not easily reversible. Cron operations affect streaks — must not trigger multiple times. Limited to personal productivity automation — no enterprise deployment. Good for self-improvement agent workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. User ID + API token dual credential — no scope granularity. x-client header required identifying your app. Community OSS maintained — dependency hygiene varies. Both User ID and API token via environment variables. Task completion and reward actions are irreversible in-game — agents need safeguards. No compliance certifications for consumer game platform.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with status page. Stable API versioning. Good backward compatibility. Retry guidance documented. Task scoring not idempotent — retrying may double-score. Cron must only trigger once per day — agents need deduplication logic. Rate limits (30/min) constrain batch operations. x-client header required — missing it may result in rate limiting. Seasonal content and events affect API behavior.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Hacker News official Firebase API — stories, comments, users, polls. No auth required — completely open read access. No rate limits published (Firebase infrastructure handles load). Items are immutable once posted. Realtime updates via Firebase streaming. Full text not available — must fetch item details individually. Top/new/best/ask/show/jobs are paginated lists. Simple, stable, free, and reliable — ideal for tech news agent workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. No credentials required or stored — zero credential management surface. Read-only public data. Y Combinator/Firebase maintained. GDPR compliance as US-hosted public data platform. No PII in API responses beyond public user profiles. Read-only operations only — no write capability via API. Excellent minimal security surface.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Hosted by Y Combinator on Firebase — excellent uptime track record. Stable API since 2014 with minimal changes. Good backward compatibility. Full GET idempotency on all queries. No rate limits — can poll freely. Individual item fetches required for full content — N+1 fetches for comment trees. Firebase streaming for real-time story monitoring. Exceptional reliability for a free API.

af_score_components.notes: Local RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) MCP server (quality 72). No auth required — local operation. Vector database + embedding for semantic search over ingested documents. No rate limits (local). Document ingestion required before querying — must populate knowledge base first. Quality depends on embedding model and chunking strategy. Supports common document formats (PDF, text, markdown). Good for building local knowledge-base agents without cloud dependencies.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local operation — no network exposure. No credentials required or stored. Read-only semantic search operations. Unknown version and dependency audit status (likely depends on vector DB library). Document contents stored locally — appropriate data handling required for sensitive documents. Local file system permissions provide implicit access control.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local tool with no SLA. Unknown version. No documented breaking change policy. Vector search queries idempotent. Document ingestion must complete before queries work. Embedding consistency required — changing embedding models requires re-ingesting all documents. Local disk space and memory constrain scale. Good for development and small-scale production use.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Harvest time tracking and invoicing API. Personal access token (PAT) is simpler for agents; OAuth2 for user-context integrations. Account ID required in all requests. Rate limits: 100 requests per 15 seconds. Time entry creation is not idempotent — retrying can create duplicate entries. Invoice management available. Project and task IDs required — must discover via list endpoints. Webhook support for time entry events. Good for automated time tracking in agent workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. PAT or OAuth2 — PAT simpler for agents. Limited scope granularity on PAT. Harvest maintained. No formal compliance certifications publicly listed beyond GDPR. PAT via environment variable. Account ID and PAT must be paired correctly. Time and invoice data is financial — appropriate data governance required. OAuth2 scopes for granular access control in multi-user deployments.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Rate limit: 100 req/15sec — design agents accordingly. Time entry creation not idempotent — check for existing entries before creating. Account ID discovery required at setup. Webhook delivery reliable with retry. Pagination uses Link headers. Invoice generation idempotent when using same parameters.
